report_name: Tata Electronics Confirms Cyberattack; Hackers Claim Leak of Apple and Tesla Data

threat_actors.name: World Leaks
threat_actors.description: A cybercriminal group that claimed responsibility for the data breach at Tata Electronics. The group is believed to be a rebrand or successor to the Hunters International ransomware group. It employed a double extortion model, demanding a ransom and subsequently leaking over 630GB of stolen data on a dark web site.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through data theft and public leaks of sensitive intellectual property.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors targeted Tata Electronics, a key supplier and trusted partner for Apple and Tesla, to indirectly access the intellectual property of these larger technology companies.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: This technique was implied by the threat actor's ransom demand, which is a key component of the double extortion model used in the attack.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely used cloud storage services to exfiltrate the large volume of 630GB of data from Tata Electronics' network.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors collected over 630GB of sensitive data from Tata's IT systems, including technical drawings, manufacturing plans, and employee PII.

attack_patterns.name: Archive Collected Data
attack_patterns.description: The use of data compression tools like 7-Zip was suggested as a likely attacker activity for staging the 630GB of data before exfiltration.

tools.name: rclone
tools.description: Legitimate data synchronization tool suggested as a hunting observable, as it is commonly abused by threat actors for large-scale data exfiltration.

tools.name: MegaCMD
tools.description: Legitimate command-line tool for the MEGA cloud storage service, suggested as a hunting observable as it is commonly abused by threat actors for data exfiltration.

tools.name: 7-Zip
tools.description: Legitimate data compression utility suggested as a likely tool used by the attackers to stage data for exfiltration.

identities.name: Tata Electronics
identities.description: A major Indian component manufacturer and key supplier for Apple, confirmed to be the victim of a cyberattack and data breach.

identities.name: Apple
identities.description: A key client of Tata Electronics whose sensitive data, including PCB designs and factory operation files, was allegedly stolen and leaked in the supply chain attack.

identities.name: Tesla
identities.description: A key client of Tata Electronics whose sensitive data, including manufacturing specifications for 'Project Highland', was allegedly stolen and leaked in the supply chain attack.

infrastructure.name: World Leaks Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the 'World Leaks' group to publish the 630GB of data stolen from Tata Electronics.

indicators.name: Abused Data Staging and Exfiltration Tool Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Abused Data Staging and Exfiltration Tool Execution
id: 2b1a9c8d-3e4f-4a5b-8c6d-7e8f9a0b1c2d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate data synchronization or compression tools like rclone, megacmd, or 7-Zip, which are commonly abused by threat actors for data staging and exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.computing.co.uk/news/2026/security/tata-electronics-confirms-cyber-breach
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.exfiltration
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rclone.exe'
      - '\megacmd.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of tools commonly used for data exfiltration and staging, as suggested in the report's hunting hints.
